llm_response:
```text
SKILL: Always obey the “ONE action in triple backticks” execution rule
- Put exactly one shell “action” in a single ```bash block.
- If you need multiple steps, wrap them into one action via: bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; cmd1; cmd2; ...'
- Avoid `&&` directly after a heredoc terminator; prefer newlines + `set -e` or wrap in bash -lc.

SKILL: Standard repo navigation + sanity checks (Bleve repo)
- Always start commands from repo root: cd /testbed
- Confirm target package path exists before edits/tests: ls analysis/token/camelcase
- Use quick symbol discovery:
  - grep -R "type CamelCaseFilter" -n analysis/token/camelcase
  - grep -R "NewCamelCaseFilter" -n /testbed
  - grep -R "registry.RegisterTokenFilter" -n analysis/token/camelcase

SKILL: Edit the existing implementation; don’t fork/duplicate it
- Prefer modifying the existing file (analysis/token/camelcase/camelcase.go) over adding a parallel file with the same exported types/functions.
- Never “fix” duplicate declarations by renaming/deleting the original source file; that can remove required init-time registration or break other tests.
- If you must refactor, move code into unexported helpers, but keep the exported API stable:
  - Keep: type CamelCaseFilter, func NewCamelCaseFilter, method Filter, and any init()/registry wiring.

SKILL: Preserve package side effects (registry/init patterns)
- In this repo, analyzers/filters often self-register in init(). When changing code:
  - Keep init() and registry.RegisterTokenFilter(...) behavior intact.
  - Ensure NewCamelCaseFilter signature and construction match what registry expects.
- After edits, verify registration references still compile by grepping for the registered name.

SKILL: Go toolchain + go.mod discipline
- Do NOT rewrite go.mod, add temporary modules, or run go get unless the task explicitly requires dependency changes.
- Assume CI/eval environment has the correct Go version for this repo; focus on making code compile under the repo’s existing go.mod/toolchain directive.
- If you see “unknown directive: toolchain” or “requires go >= X”, the correct fix is usually “use the repo-required Go version”, not “replace go.mod”.

SKILL: Test-running strategy (fast to full)
- First run focused package tests:
  - go test ./analysis/token/camelcase -v
- Then run the full suite to catch cross-package breaks:
  - go test ./... -v
- Treat compile errors in other packages as a signal you changed or removed required API/registration.

SKILL: Debugging camelCase boundary bugs (byte offsets vs runes)
- In bleve tokens, Start/End are byte offsets. If iterating over runes:
  - Track explicit byte indices (using utf8.DecodeRune) and compute Start/End as token.Start + byteOffset.
- Add tests that assert both token terms AND Start/End boundaries, including at least one UTF-8 multi-byte input.

SKILL: Safe file writing patterns from shell
- When writing files via shell, use:
  - cat > path/file.go <<'EOF'
  - (no trailing spaces after EOF)
  - Never chain heredoc blocks with `&&`; separate with newlines.
- After code edits, always run gofmt on changed .go files:
  - gofmt -w analysis/token/camelcase/*.go

SKILL: “Definition conflicts” checklist
- If you see “redeclared in this block”:
  - grep for the symbol across the package, identify duplicates, and merge into a single implementation.
  - Do NOT rename .go files to .bak to “make it pass”; instead remove the new duplicate or integrate changes into the original file.

SKILL: Before finalizing, confirm repository integrity
- Ensure you didn’t leave backup files that might confuse tooling or reviewers (e.g., *.go.bak).
- Ensure package compiles without relying on local environment hacks; rerun go test ./... from /testbed.
```
